comments.title: Crackers and Cheese
comments.content:
Hi JMG,
Three days ago, my husband was teasing me in the morning. He said the house elf had a little party the night before. I didn't understand what he meant. He said there were pieces of crackers and crumbs of cheese all over the floor in the living room.

I had gone to bed early the night before and the living room was clean and orderly when I left it. I had not had a snack. He had not had a snack. The doors were all locked. We are the only people who live in this house.

I had a look and there were indeed pieces of cracker and crumbs of cheese strew all over the floor by the sofa in front of the fire.

In addition, about ten days ago, we woke up in the very early morning to the fire alarm. We raced around the house looking for the fire. We could smell something singed. It turns out that the electric base-board heater next to our bed had been turned up all the way. We have neither of us used that heater for over a decade. I had heard strange rustling noises coming from it the night before and sat up to look but could see nothing. Someone had turned the knob to put the heater on full.

I did some divinations and the divinations indicated it was a neighbour who had died a few weeks before of cancer. He liked the heat turned way up.

Now, he had not been able to eat much due to surgeries for his cancer. Have you ever heard of a ghost eating, or at least trying to eat food?


I frankincensed the house today as part of my Alban Arthuan ceremony. I do not think our neighbour is at all harmful but was not sure it was really him. Do you have any advice to offer?
Maxine

replies.title: Re: Crackers and Cheese
replies.content: Yes, ghosts will quite often try to fulfill in death what they could not do in life, such as have a good meal while dying of cancer, or getting their environment hot enough. Talk to him and let him know what the problems are with his recent action; ghosts are generally in something very like a dream state, and therefore are suggestible.

Maxine

replies.content: There are quite a few; I'll use the Greek pantheon for examples, because most people know it fairly well, but you can adapt it to fit the pantheon of your choice. Zeus as the ruler of the gods was always invoked to bless a nation. Athena is the goddess of civilized life and so is responsive to prayers asking for politicians and people alike to be responsive to their civic duties. replies.title: Re: "Insurance gap"
replies.content:
Saint Columba’s Caim/Circle Prayer

The Celtic Church developed a special type of protection prayer, the Caim Prayer or Circle Prayer. This was a way to surround oneself with the protection and blessing of the Triune God.

To perform the Caim Prayer, you stand up, point your right index finger at the ground and turn in a clockwise circle. At certain positions you will say prayers.

Imagining your circle is a clock face at the:
• 12 position, “Circle me/us Lord, Keep protection near, And danger afar.”
• 3 position, “Circle me/us Lord, Keep light near, And darkness afar.”
• 6 position, “Circle me/us Lord, Keep peace within, Keep evil out.”
• 9 position, “Circle me/us Lord, Keep hope within, Keep doubt without.”
• Back at the 12 position can finish your prayers with:
May you be a bright flame before me/us
May you be a guiding star above me/us,
May you be a smooth path below me/us,
And a loving Guide behind me/us,
Today, tonight, and forever. Amen.

This was passed onto me by a friend.
Source: https://jtalexanderiv.wordpress.com/

comments.title: DIY or Otherwise
comments.content:
Hi JMG, I'm curious as to what circumstances you think make sense for purchasing magical/spiritual services from others. Many people in the occult trades offer to dress candles, do spells and divinations for you and more. I've had little interest in such offerings, as I think that the goal of the path should be to develop the skill to do these things for yourself. Otherwise you could end up with a dead-end transactional approach to magic, kind of like the people who prompted you to ban questions about asking spirits for things. I've purchased equipment I can't readily make by myself like divination tools and altar objects; I see building all my own equipment as an ideal to approach but not something I'm likely to do anytime soon.

Do you think buying services and tools from others can be helpful? How would you draw the line between procuring a service that can help you on your path and attempting to charge your magical progress to a credit card?

replies.title: Re: DIY or Otherwise
replies.content: It's very much a matter of intention and attitude. I buy items I need fairly often, when I don't have the skills to make them myself or when the investment in time, tools, etc. would become a barrier to something else of higher importance. I also get services from time to time -- for example, even if you're a good astrologer, it's useful to have someone else analyze your horoscope, as they'll see things you don't. The point, to my mind, is whether the purchase furthers your own work on yourself or whether it's a substitute for work on yourself.

comments.title: Dogs and the Materialist Tripwire
comments.content:
Hey JMG,

A blessed solstice to you too!

1) Is there any good bit of natural magic you'd recommend for repelling and/or pacifying aggressive dogs? I have now been charged by two different dogs in my local forest and while the owners were correct in saying that they don't bite, this still doesn't strike me as a particularly adequate response—leashing the dog (as would be required by law anyways) instead of just leisurely walking past me and the little demon would seem like a good start. Either way, I'd like to do something about it, preferably something that won't land me in court for animal cruelty.

2) I'd like to confirm my understanding of prayer with you: somewhere out there on the divine plane, there are the Gods. In prayer, they or their attending spirits communicate with us on some lower plane.

That communication happens partly in words, partly in images, and partly in emotions, presumably in different ratios for everyone. How clearly we perceive these depends on how noisy our own current mental and emotional state is, not because the Gods couldn't work around that but because they largely choose not to.

That would imply that we are somehow involved in perceiving the message. My guess, and this might be completely nonsensical, is that communication (mostly) happens with the mental sheath and we need to translate the message to the astral plane ourselves.

That, in turn, implies the possibility for mistranslation. Not only can we misunderstand the symbols on the astral, there's not even a guarantee that the symbols match up with what we're meant to perceive. If we're in a properly receptive state, they'll be most likely correct, but if we're not, we might just see what we expect to see, a curiously goLLuM-like completion of the message perceived so far.

3) If this is largely correct, I think I discovered a mechanism that I'd call the materialist tripwire.

Pop culture is inundated with caricatures of the Gods, behaving in thoroughly ungodly fashion¹. Positive qualities are perverted, e.g. Thor turns from the fierce protector of Midgard into a cruel, oafish figure that delights in inflicting suffering. We would expect these elements to show up in the non-receptive "prayer completion" of those of us who have consumed too much pop culture before we knew better.

At that point, any sane person should recognise that it's pretty likely they're not talking to Thor, for the simple reason that he would have been feared, not revered, if he actually behaved this way². This is where the materialist tripwire comes in: societal programming will helpfully remind you that hearing voices is a sign of schizophrenia (nevermind the fact that inner dialogue is a well-known phenomenon).

If successful, the tripwire will have set the person praying on the path of doubting not just (correctly) the current message but also (wrongly) all prior religious experiences and thus his own sanity. The ultimate goal is to get him first to a psychiatrist and then on antipsychotics as soon as possible, safeguarding materialism from criticism.

—David P.

[1] If that phenomenon isn't actively encouraged by demons, they must be laughing their, ahem, bottoms off. In a rather memorable recent conversation, a (not particularly pious) acquaintance made a case for believing in the Christian God along the lines of Pascal's wager. Hoping for a serious theological discussion, I pointed out that there might be several Gods. He thought about that for a moment and replied "then I'd believe in Aphrodite—she's got big [breasts]." We did not continue the conversation.

[2] In fact, one argument my Catholic RE teacher brought against polytheism is that polytheists Gods are "just like humans." I suppose that this is what happens when you don't try to understand myths as myths, though I wonder how literal the average peasant would have taken them.

replies.title: Re: Dogs and the Materialist Tripwire
replies.content:
1) I wish I did. I was severely mauled by a dog when I was six, and have had to deal with a gut-level distrust of dogs ever since.

2) That's more or less correct, though the answer generally shows up on the upper levels of the astral as well -- thus the strong emotional component.

3) Good heavens, yes. Materialism, like all ideologies, has various gimmicks to defend itself from criticism, and that's one of them.

Note 1 -- Aphrodite's endowments were in fact an object of worship back in the day. The gap between sexuality and spirituality is nothing like as wide as most Piscean religions like to claim. Anything that inspires awe and strong emotion...

Note 2 -- The Christian god is vulnerable to the same argument, of course. I've pointed out before that in Catholic theology, he's presented as a cosmic domestic abuser.

replies.content:

Re Note 1: Then maybe it's just my Piscean biases showing, but I would assume that even in that case, a certain amount of reverence would be called for that that remark was sorely lacking. I suppose, though, that that's for Aphrodite herself to decide and none of my business.

Re Note 2: Of course. To be fair, if I recall correctly, said teacher thought of hell more along the lines of "absence of God that you have to consciously choose" and not "flaming lakes in which you will be tortured for all eternity if you hold hands before marriage."

—David P.

replies.title: Re: Dogs
replies.content:
We have a somewhat similar situation with the dogs in our neck of the woods, I just solve it in a projective way.
Dog A was a problem on our walks when my partner was pregnant. Truth be told her whole family has a problem with dogs. Once late last year while walking with her parents this dog ran up to the group and started jumping up her father and actually nipping at his heals and fingers. They were all scared, and complaining when they came back.
Later in the month we, just the two of us, went the sam way, but much later in the day, it was dark and cold and the dog was barking from the shadows. She was frightened. And I told her to just keep walking, nothing is going to happen. I set myself between her and the dog, thought to myself “he is a 40 kg mutt, I am more then twice his weight and in a thick winter coat. He gets one bite at the sleeve then his ribs and throat break”. As the dog came closer I just looked at him and calmly told him, that he is a good guardian and we are just walking trough. The dog never got more then 10 meters towards me.

Dog B; I like to call “mirror dog” because he obviously mirrors peoples moods and intentions. I noticed when I was taking a walk and ruminating over some anger and dark thoughts, he ran out furiously barking, but calmed down, when I greeted him. Conversely, when I bless him from afar and am in a positive mood, he comes with a wagging tail and happy disposition.

JMG, is it possible to communicate with animals in this fashion? I noticed animals, dogs, and cows responding to my gestures followed by intend, like pointing to a heard of driven cattle to go down the left path and meaning it.

Oh, and I am imagining you roaring in rage. Oh my. My deepest sympathies to the poor Doberman. My reaction would probably be much the same.

replies.title: Re: Dogs
replies.content:
1) Yes, and in fact that's the one way you can communicate with most mammals. They understand human body language. Dog A will have realized that you were prepared to kill it, and so backed off hard. The other animals will have picked up on your intention, as expressed by body language, and thought, "Well, this one at least knows how to talk."

2) The Doberman certainly seemed to be impressed by it. Sara used to laugh hard, remembering that incident.

replies.title: Re: Dogs

—David P.

replies.title: Re: Dogs
replies.content:
It is my observation that guard dog breeds such as Dobermans, Rottweilers, German Shepherds, etc. tend to have what I call a "police attitude." Like an alert human officer, if they sense that their presence makes you nervous, they seem to feel that you might be up to something and need watching. I've also noticed that some big dogs seem confused by my lack of response (I've been around a lot of big dogs). They are so used to people being nervous that they are taken aback by someone who is merely cautions. And don't forget that the "joke" remark "they can smell fear" is true.

Rita

replies.title: Re: Dogs and the Materialist Tripwire
replies.content:
It's not magical David, but this worked for me:
I had two vicious dogs who would jump up and snap at me over a low fence on my daily walk. I began to carry a pouch of "Begging Strips" (Bacon flavoured dog snacks) with me. When the vicious dogs came, I threw each of them a begging strip. In a week, they were wagging their tails and sitting up for treats.
When the owner complained, I simply told him, "If you don't want me feeding your dogs, keep them leashed and away from the sidewalk."

comments.title: (no subject)
comments.content:
Dear JMG,

Although I'm working through RTOL, I was up until recently doing Ogham divination. After years of doing daily Ogham divination for myself ("what do I need to know about the coming day?"), I recently realized I've developed a bad habit.

Background: I have been getting more accurate with the Ogham, for example, if I do a one-card reading for a yes/no question, I will fairly often have a sense of yes/no before I flip the few over to look at it. The Ogham will sometimes predict events in rough terms (like a tendency or category under which the events of the day fall into), but it is most often predicting states of consciousness that I will be under that day, and it will do so with some accuracy.

In so doing, though, I realized that I often prime myself for a certain outcome as a result of reading the fews. For example, if I pull reversed fews for all three cards of the daily spread, I'll think I'm about to have a rough day, and then when I do have a rough day, I'll get a sense that it happened because I expected it. I remember from the DMH that useful concept of intentionality: I had the intention to have whatever day the Ogham predicted.

In that sense, the Ogham didn't predict the events that led to me having some trouble, it set a frame that I used to perceive events in a certain way, like a kind of confirmation bias.

I'm sure this is a common issue among diviners. How do you handle this?

As an experiment, for the past two or three weeks, I've stopped doing daily divination and I find it liberating. I'm taking each moment as it is and not getting fatalistic about it.

Thanks for any input, and I hope you had a blessed winter solstice!

replies.title: (no subject)
replies.content: This is one of the reasons why some occult teachers don't recommend divination at the beginner level. I personally find it useful to focus each daily reading on what I can do with the energies of the day, whatever those happen to be -- thus it's not a matter of fatalism, but of exploring options. But your mileage may vary, of course.

replies.title: Re: Saturn-Neptune Conjunction
replies.content:
It's the most important astrological event of our lifetimes, and a hundred lifetimes to either side. replies.title: Re: Don Juan

I enjoyed de Mille's book -- that's the source of the distinction I often draw between the authenticity of a system (whether it has the origins it claims) and its validity (whether it has the results it claims). replies.title: (no subject)
replies.content: This is one of the places where the philosophy of ethics has gotten hopelessly confused by centuries of political religion. Go back two millennia and it's much clearer. The word "vice" comes from Latin vitium, literally "weakness," just as "virtue" comes from virtus, which literally means "manly strength" -- it's from the same root as "virility"! Your vices are your weaknesses, the things that make it easier for others to dominate and exploit you. Your virtues are your strengths, the things that enable you to meet challenges and accomplish your will. Your job, in crafting the two soul mirrors, is to figure out which of your characteristics weaken you and which strengthen you. It's not an easy or a simple task!

The story doesn't say enough about the Breton site for me to guess what it was for, but it was doubtless raised by the same people who put up the standing stones in the same region.

replies.title: (no subject)
replies.content:
Fascinating! Thanks for this.

They weren't speaking Gaelic, though -- that's spoken in Ireland and Scotland. Breton, Brezhoneg to those who speak it, is an independent Celtic language, closely related to Cornish and a little more distantly akin to Welsh; a Breton speaker and a Gaelic speaker, if they don't know the other's language, can't understand a word the other says. A Breton speaker saying "Good morning. How are you?" would say, "Demat dit. Mat an traoù ganeoc’h?" A Gaelic speaker would say the same thing as "Maidin mhaith. Conas atá tú?"

comments.title: Dedicating a shring to Gaia on the solstice
comments.content:
Happy belated winter solstice! I would like to share with you a yuletide tale of how I soent my solstice.

So, I have a dedicated shrine to Jupiter, who I consider to be my patron, but I very mich believe in balance in all things, and that the divine masculine ahould be balanced by the divine feminine, and their energies both worked with. So, for some time I have intended to dedicate a shrine to Gaia on my land.

I found the right spot, and began levelling and raising the land on friday, which divination led me to believe was the correct day for this. I originally intended to do the dedication Friday as I had plans the rest of the week, but the gods and godesses have a way of making a mockery of the plans of mortals.

So, yesterday I had a positively herculean task to perform - I'd discovered fleas in my home, and I cannot tolerate that. So, while I do love nature and hate using poison, I decided to bug bomb my house and take out the cats and flea bathe them afyer giving them flea treatment. Before bug bombing the house, I had to take out basically everything. The only person who could help me was deathly ill.

I tell you what, nothing will make you aware of how much extra junk you have or more determined to downsize than having to move everything you own. It took all day. We didn't get out until sundown of the longest night of the year. A chill fell over me as I had finally emptied my home of all material posessions. I bathed the cats at another home devoid of carpeting or furniture - its about to be sold - and then left my partner in crime to sleep on an inflatable mattress while I returned home.

The bombs were still going off, and I had turned off all power to the house so nothing ended up in the vents or fridge. It was dark and cold, no light but the stars. I decided to start a fire, to warm me and provide light during this dark time, reminding me of the old tradition of keeping a fire going through the night of the solstice.

As I sat by the fire, thinking over all the work I had done, all that I had to do, feeling stressed and overwhelmed, a realization came to me, and it sounded like a female voice within my mind. "The nature of this material Earth makes struggle and work unavoidable if you would impose your will. This is the nature of the Earth. She is stubborn and does not yield. It is the source of her strength and endurance."

It put my great efforts in a new light, and immediately had me think of Gaia. I took a small tea candle and wandered back to my shrine - not far from the fire, which is in a oit and I could keep an eye on - and sat and meditated. I realized that I had misrepresented Gaia in my mind. She is often pictured by hippie leftist occult types as all loving, benevolent, giving. And all these are true... but only half the picture. They also view her as this helpless maiden being ravaged by cruel humanity, giving us all the power. But the truth is, Mother Earth is tough as nails - literally, she's mostly iron. She pushes back constantly, stubbornly refuses to yield, and can be violent and destructive when the mood takes her. The Greeks were closer to nature and understood her better - mother and creator of all, but also destroyer, empowering her chipd Saturn to castrate his father, then raising Jupiter to overthrow him in turn. Mother of all, including monsters, and ultimately destroyer of us all. There's a Saturn like side to her - which makes sense, as she is his mother. But also, the hardness of her material nature is what provides our lives with structure and makes our accomplishments have meaning.

After a time of meditation, I returned to my fire, but I knew what I was going to do. The earth is of winter and the north, and this day of winter I would dedicate her north facing shring. On this night of darkness I would honor the earth, who conceives all in darkness.

Of course, I still had work to do. I opened the doors of my house to let it air out, everything still dark and cold. I cleansed my home, cleaning every surface, every dirty corner expoaed by my lack of clutter, and marvelled at the beautiy if the simplicity. I saged the whole house while I was at it, driving out both fleas and chemicals. It occured to me that we were all kind of fleas on the back of mother earth, and I was glad she hadn't decided to scratch us off.

Later, after I turned the power back on, turned on every fan, thoroughly aired out the house, I set to vaccuming and steam cleaning. Eventually my other roomates came home and I bribed them to finish the task. I kept the fire going and then sat once more by my shrine in meditation. I felt a gratitude from Gaia, for seeing and honoring her as she is, and not as a projection of some image of an innocent virgin mary substitute. My struggles that day had been a part of me awakening to the inevitability of struggle when dealing with the material world.

Later, when the house was clean, cats were back, our stuff mostly loaded back in, it was almost midnight. Exhauste but determined, I gathered olive oil and wine, took my holiday wreath of pine and holly off the front door, and went to the shrine. I officially invoked Gaia, laid the wreath on her shrine, offered oblations of oil and wine, sang her orphic hymn, and swore to honor her and be a respectful steward of the little piece of her the law called mine, though I knew I belonged to the land. In my cold midnight meditations, she seemed well pleased and accepted my offering and would come to my shrine.

There's my solstice story. Hope you all enjoyed.

comments.content:
Hello!

Last week, I posted a question about country egregores and making a decision about whether to emigrate, and you responded that two of my three questions would be best answered with divination. I happened to take that as my cue to finally get off of my behind and start picking up divination in earnest, as I'd been meaning to for quite some time, and so I spent the past week studying the Sacred Geometry Oracle. As you guessed, I originally asked the question because I had a major life decision coming up that involved potentially moving out of the country.

And so, well, I've cast my first reading, but as a complete novice, I wanted a second pair of eyes to make sure I'm not way off in my interpretations or anything. Hopefully this is okay? Apologies in advance, I asked the oracle a lot of questions.

To summarize my situation in brief, I am living in America and after a recent visit, I'm considering moving to another country that I won't name. I figured I had decent prospects for finding work in my current field in that country, and I think I would enjoy the culture, learning the language, and the reduced cost of living. However, this would involve changing my current plans, which are to stay where I am in America, with family, living on very little expenses, and focusing on a business venture I've been planning for a while. I also have chronic health issues, and I wonder if it is wise to start over somewhere else without any familial support given this fact.

I have adequate financial stability here if I stay with family, which means I can pursue my venture more freely. On the other hand, moving means my visa would be tied to my employer, and along with the other concerns of immigration, it's quite possible my business would have to be put on the backburner for a few, or even many years. Either way, there's an opportunity cost, so I'm having trouble weighing the options and deciding on a course of action.


So for the SGO, I did two sessions: one for moving, and one for staying. These were the questions I asked and the results:

Moving to the new country:

1. Myself - Equilateral Triangle reversed (Limitation)
2. My relationship with my family - Quadrature Circle reversed (Isolation)
3. Relationship with a close friend - Icosahedron reversed (Passivity)
4. Prospects for a job in my current field - The Point (Beginning)
5. What I stand to gain by moving - Ellipse (Flow)
6. What I stand to lose by moving - Hexagram (Balance)
7. What I stand to lose by staying - Discontinuous Proportion (Relation)
8. My best approach - Right Triangle reversed (Restriction)

Follow-up questions:

1. Prospects for getting a visa with a different approach - Golden Proportion (Harmony)
2. Prospects for permanent residency - Progression of Roots (Evolution)
3. How well my health will hold up - Square and Diagonal reversed (Consequences)
4. How well I will take to the language - Dodecagram reversed (Complexity)
5. How well I will adapt to the culture - Circle reversed (Repetition)
6. Romantic prospects - Continuous Proportion reversed (Necessity)
7. Friendship prospects - Octagram reversed (Compromise)
8. Business's prospects for success in the new country - Sphere (Infinity)
9. Opportunities for creative growth - Pentagram (Power)

So the number of reverse cards in this reading initially spooked me, but upon reading into the meanings it doesn't seem to be as negative as my first impression suggested. However, it seems I will struggle with the language and culture more than I anticipated, and the Limitation reading on Self, Isolation reading on family, Restriction reading for best approach, and especially the Consequences reading for health suggest to me that moving may not be possible or would be very unwise at this time?

Golden Proportion for my visa and Progression of Roots for my permanent residency suggest that another pathway may open to me with time that will be more harmonious with my overall life trajectory. Also, what I stand to gain, business success, and opportunities for creative growth all turn up positive, to my eye, so that means I shouldn't discount moving to this country entirely? Discontinuous Proportion for what I stand to lose by not moving seems to support this as well, I'm interpreting that to mean that experiences and relationships I am not yet aware of would be sacrificed if I never make the journey?

The Romance reading is a little confusing to me, is it saying I won't have prospects at this time and I just have to deal with that or is it saying my prospects are what they are and I just have to deal with that?

Here's the reading for staying and focusing on the business:

1. Myself - Cube reversed (Rigidity)
2. Relationship with Family - Sphere (Infinity)
3. Relationship with a close friend - Spiral reversed (Diminishing)
4. Business Prospects for Success - Golden Proportion (Harmony)
5. What I stand to gain by staying - Dodecagram (Completeness)
6. What I stand to lose by staying - Circle (Continuity)
7. What I stand to lose by not staying - Continuous Proportion (Correspondence)
8. My best approach - Transcendence (Dodecahedron)

Follow-up questions:

1. Romantic prospects - The Point (Beginning)
2. Opportunities for creative growth - Square reversed (Inertia)
3. How my health will hold up - Cross reversed (Dissension)
4. Friendship prospects - Human Canon reversed (Diversity)
5. Other possibilities that are open to me - Equilateral Triangle (Form)
6. How I should pursue these possibilities - Hexagram (Balance)
7. Prospects for homeownership in the future - The Line reversed (Separation)
8. Prospects for homeownership if I move after all - Progression of Roots (Evolution)

So overall, to my untrained eye, a much more positive reading, or at least there are fewer reverse cards. It seems to be saying that my worst enemy in this scenario will be myself and my old habits. To the extent that I can embrace spiritual growth and think outside of the box I've been raised in, I will meet with a great deal of success (health reading is still bad but I'm interpreting that as "yeah, it's a bad situation and you'll just have to deal with it"?) but I'll have to give up on my hopes for homeownership in the near future.

Taken together, the two readings seem to be saying, "at this time, you should stay and focus on your business. Better opportunities for living in that country will open up to you in the future."

So, my questions:

1. Does my reading seem more or less correct? Is the first reading more negative than the second or am I misinterpreting it? Any other necessary corrections you see?

2. If you got this reading and you were hypothetically looking to move, what would you take away from it?

3. It seems like the Continuous Proportion card is especially difficult for me to interpret. Can you explain what is meant by the reversed reading for Romance in Scenario 1, and the standard reading for "What I stand to lose by not staying" in Scenario 2? Cross reversed (Dissension) for health in Scenario 2 is also a little confusing, and Equilateral Triangle (Form) for other possibilities also feels a little vague. If you could clarify those as well, I'd be very appreciative.

4. Anything else I should keep in mind or know about?

Apologies for the long length, and thank you for your time and attention. If you do actually help me interpret all of this, I promise to leave a tip in one of the jars.
